mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, increasing present values and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day S&P 500 forward returns is positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market deterioration and collapsing aggregate demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in retail sales and industrial production indicate a broad demand-supply contraction that depresses corporate revenues and margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint negative shocks to RSAFS and INDPRO fail to produce more negative 20-day S&P 500 returns than either series alone over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of weak output growth, reductions in the federal funds rate provide liquidity relief that dominates default-risk concerns, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: In quarters with GDPC1 growth below 1%, falling FEDFUNDS coincides with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month surge in industrial production above 0.5% signals expanding operating leverage and improving earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 55% of weeks when INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive monthly declines in housing starts foreshadow falling construction activity, household wealth destruction, and tightening mortgage credit, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Three consecutive monthly declines in HOUST are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating nonfarm payrolls growth signals rising aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS fail to predict positive 20-day S&P 500 returns with a rank IC above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds the nominal federal funds rate, negative real interest rates erode cash and fixed-income values, redirecting capital into equities and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Instances where CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months.
